title: The Toolkit - Week 1
subject: The AI tools that actually saved time this week — and 2 that didn't
preheader: Notion AI, Otter.ai, Reclaim AI, and Grammarly put to the test
bodyHtml:
The Toolkit

AI Productivity Hub · Week of April 7, 2026

Welcome to the first issue of The Toolkit — your weekly roundup of AI productivity tools that actually deliver. Each week we test tools in real workflows and tell you what’s worth your time. Let’s get into it.

Notion AI — The All-in-One That Actually Delivers

We ran a full project setup — meeting notes, task boards, docs — entirely inside Notion AI. The Q&A feature across your workspace is genuinely useful: it pulled context from three different docs to answer a planning question in seconds. Where it struggles: the AI writing suggestions still feel generic compared to a dedicated tool. Verdict: keep it for knowledge work, skip it for copywriting. Read our full review →

Otter.ai — Meeting Notes on Autopilot

We recorded five meetings across Zoom and Google Meet. Otter.ai nailed speaker identification 90% of the time and the auto-generated summaries were accurate enough to send directly to stakeholders. The real win: action item extraction. It caught commitments we missed. Verdict: a genuine time-saver if you’re in 3+ meetings a day. Read our full review →

Reclaim AI — Calendar Magic (With Caveats)

Reclaim promises to auto-schedule tasks, habits, and buffer time. In practice, it worked brilliantly for a solo calendar. But with shared team calendars and external meetings, it kept rescheduling deep work blocks into 7 AM slots nobody wants. Verdict: excellent for solopreneurs, needs work for teams.

Grammarly — Still the Gold Standard for Business Writing

Grammarly’s AI rewrite feature has gotten noticeably better. We tested it on 20 cold outreach emails and the tone adjustments were spot-on — professional without sounding robotic. The browser extension still occasionally conflicts with Google Docs formatting, but it’s minor. Verdict: worth the premium for anyone writing client-facing content. Read our full review →

This Week’s Quick Pick

ChatGPT vs Claude for Work

We tested both AI assistants on 10 real business tasks. The results were surprisingly split. See which one won for email drafting, data analysis, and creative brainstorming.

Read the Comparison →

That’s Week 1. Got a tool you want us to test? Reply to this email — we read every one.
